notes: DDR5-6400 over the last 7-14 days stayed anchored by 64GB and 128GB, with 96GB only recently repaired and 32GB still weaker on exact public anchors.
DDR5-5600 remained structurally split at 32GB for the full review window; this is persistently outlier_only rather than no_public_sample.
PCIe4 TLC remained the most stable and executable category, with 30.72TB public coverage proving durable after the 2026-04-09 recovery.
PCIe5 TLC kept decent nominal coverage, but 30.72TB anchor decay stayed the key unresolved issue: the old lower-band reference has not returned cleanly since 2026-04-13.
PCIe4 QLC stayed fully covered, but part of the supplementation burden shifted from clean public pages to parser-gap and RFQ classification at Mouser, PCNation, and Wiredzone.

noDirectSampleSources: 16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
16GB exact comparable part still not recovered
outlierOnlySources: A-Tech/Newegg/CDW Kingston low cluster vs Provantage/CDW Micron high cluster for 32GB

noDirectSampleSources: Provantage Kioxia 30.72TB lower-band exact public tile still missing

keyChangesVsYesterday: No tracked representative value changed versus 2026-04-16; the market still looks like stable main bands plus unresolved structural splits.
DDR5-5600 32GB was rechecked and remains clearly outlier_only rather than no_public_sample: low retail pages and high distributor pages both remain visible.
PCIe5 TLC 30.72TB still failed to recover the older lower-band anchor cleanly, so stale_repeated_sample remains the right explanation.
QLC supplementation work again showed that Mouser and PCNation are better classified as parser_gap / access problems than as evidence of no public market pricing.

DDR5-6400.reason: 64GB and 128GB remain stable, 96GB remains visible after the recent recovery, but 32GB still lacks a clean exact public anchor and 16GB is still absent.

DDR5-5600.reason: 32GB is still structurally split across low retail and high distributor bands, while 64GB and 128GB remain visible but are not at attractive low levels.

PCIe4 TLC.reason: This remains the most stable and fully covered category, with repeatable public distribution bands and no new sign of anchor decay.

PCIe5 TLC.reason: Lower capacities remain observable, but 30.72TB still lacks a trustworthy single representative public band because the old lower anchor remains stale while reproducible Micron high-band quotes persist.

PCIe4 QLC.reason: Main premium public bands remain usable, but high-capacity low clusters are still too noisy to justify a one-shot bottom-call.

gaps.note: Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Today's multi-source checks still did not recover a clean enterprise single-stick public quote.
Low-side public pages remain far below the higher distributor band, so the slot still cannot support a single representative tracking value.
The previously preferred lower-band anchor still did not repeat cleanly; current evidence remains split between a sold-out low cluster and reproducible Micron high-band quotes.
